summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the correct name for CaBr₂?

Calcium bromide is correct. This is a key concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for KBr?

Potassium bromide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for CuCl₂?

Copper(II) chloride. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for FeO?

Iron(II) oxide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for Al₂S₃?

Aluminum sulfide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for ZnO?

Zinc oxide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for PbO₂?

Lead(IV) oxide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for Cr₂O₃?

Chromium(III) oxide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for AgCl?

Silver chloride. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name for MnO₂?

Manganese(IV) oxide. This is an important concept in Naming Ionic Compounds.

What is the name of SnO₂?

SnO₂: 2 O²⁻ = −4; Sn must be +4 = Sn⁴⁺. Tin has variable charges → Roman numeral: tin(IV) oxide.

What is the formula for cobalt(III) nitride?

Co³⁺ and N³⁻: charges equal and opposite → 1 of each. Formula: CoN. (Verify: +3 + −3 = 0.)

What is the name of Cu₂S?

Cu₂S: S²⁻ (−2); 2 Cu ions → total +2 → each Cu = +1 = Cu⁺. Copper has variable charge → Roman numeral: copper(I) sulfide.

What is the formula for iron(II) sulfate?

Iron(II) = Fe²⁺. Sulfate = SO₄²⁻. Equal and opposite charges: 1 Fe²⁺ + 1 SO₄²⁻ → FeSO₄.

What is the formula for silver nitrate?

Ag⁺ (silver, fixed +1) + NO₃⁻ (nitrate, −1): 1:1 ratio → AgNO₃.

What is the name of Hg₂Cl₂?

Hg₂Cl₂: 2 Cl⁻ = −2; Hg₂²⁺ ion (both Hg atoms together = +2, each = +1). Mercury(I) chloride. Note: Hg₂²⁺ is a unique diatomic mercury cation.
